title: draba cinerea subsp. dovrensis
intro: Draba cinerea subsp. dovrensis, also known as Dovre Draba, is a rare sub-species of the Draba cinerea plant, which belongs to the mustard family and is a type of small, flowering plant that grows in the wild. The plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which are attributed to the presence of various bioactive compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Dovre Draba are said to include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and have antimicrobial properties, making it a potential natural remedy for various health issues. Herbal preparations of Dovre Draba typically involve drying the plant material, then using it to make teas, infusions, or tinctures that can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
